name: Castanyer Gros de Can Cuch and Vallforners Reservoir Circular
name_original: Pantano y riera de Vallforners, Castanyer de Can Cuc, Cànoves  – Ruta Circular
description: Hike a moderate 8.2-mile (13.2 km) circular route to see the ancient Castanyer Gros de Can Cuch and the tranquil Vallforners Reservoir.
region_intro: Hiking around Cànoves i Samalús offers access to the diverse landscapes at the foot of the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region features a mosaic of terrain, including ancient forests, the tranquil Vallforners Reservoir, and the Riera de Vallforners stream. Notable peaks like Roca Centella (1,001 m) and El Sui (1,319 m) define the mountainous backdrop, with lower hills and wooded areas dominated by holm oaks and pines. This area provides varied hiking opportunities through its natural features. Best hiking trails around Cànoves i Samalús The most popular hiking route is Castanyer Gros de Can Cuch and Vallforners Reservoir Circular, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route visits the ancient Castanyer Gros de Can Cuch and follows the banks of the Vallforners Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Riera de Vallforners Trail, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This trail winds through the Montseny Natural Park, following the Riera de Vallforners and its riverside vegetation. Local hikers also love the From the Embalse de de Vallforners to the ermita de Sant Salvador — circular, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail leading through forested areas and past the reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Cànoves i Samalús is defined by its ancient forests, the Vallforners Reservoir, and the foothills of the Montseny Natural Park. The network offers options for easy family strolls, moderate forest paths, and more challenging ascents. The routes in Cànoves i Samalús are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cànoves i Samalús's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Cànoves i Samalús?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, from gentle plains to ancient forests and mountain peaks. You'll find wooded areas dominated by holm oaks and pines, with oaks, chestnuts, and beeches at higher elevations. The area is at the foot of the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, providing varied terrain for all hiking levels.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options in Cànoves i Samalús?
A: Yes, Cànoves i Samalús offers several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallforners Stream – Vallforners Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.9 km hike that takes about 1 hour 27 minutes. The fluvial walk along the Riera de Vallforners is also suitable for families, offering a pleasant stroll along the stream.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or attractions I can visit on a hike?
A: You can explore several significant natural landmarks. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949241408/castanyer-gros-de-can-cuch-and-vallforners-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castanyer Gros de Can Cuch&lt;/a&gt;, a monumental chestnut tree over 1,000 years old, is a popular destination. The tranquil Vallforners Reservoir is also a central feature, with routes along its banks. For impressive views, consider hikes towards peaks like Roca Centella (1,001 m).

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Cànoves i Samalús are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949241408/castanyer-gros-de-can-cuch-and-vallforners-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castanyer Gros de Can Cuch and Vallforners Reservoir Circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.2 km route. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352065630/from-the-embalse-de-de-vallforners-to-the-ermita-de-sant-salvador-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Embalse de Vallforners to the Ermita de Sant Salvador — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.7 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cànoves i Samalús?
A: The mild Mediterranean climate, tempered by the surrounding pre-coastal mountains, ensures pleasant conditions for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for hiking, with lush vegetation in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, with typical droughts, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the Montseny Natural Park offers ascents to higher peaks. Within the immediate area, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352066502/ascent-to-the-peak-of-centelles-circular-from-the-vallforners-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the peak of Centelles — circular from the Vallforners reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.9 km route with significant elevation gain. Further into Montseny, peaks like Matagalls (1,697 m), Turó de l'Home (1,706 m), and Les Agudes (1,703 m) provide rewarding, more strenuous climbs with panoramic views.

Q: Can I find waterfalls along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area around Cànoves i Samalús features some beautiful waterfalls. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1478633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of Sot del Bac&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2757495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Sort&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable natural features in the nearby gorges and canyons.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cànoves i Samalús?
A: The hiking routes in Cànoves i Samalús are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the forests, and the well-maintained paths that lead to natural landmarks like the Vallforners Reservoir and ancient trees.

Q: How many hiking routes are there in the Cànoves i Samalús area?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes available around Cànoves i Samalús, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 5 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and 7 more difficult trails, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced trekkers.

Q: Are there any specific trails that follow the Riera de Vallforners stream?
A: Yes, the Riera de Vallforners stream, which flows through the municipality, offers scenic hiking opportunities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352063277/riera-de-vallforners-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Vallforners Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.8 km path that winds through the Montseny Natural Park, following the stream and showcasing its interesting riverside vegetation.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: As Cànoves i Samalús is located at the foot of the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, the area is rich in biodiversity. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, you might encounter various bird species, small mammals, and diverse flora typical of Mediterranean mountain environments, including holm oaks, pines, oaks, chestnuts, and beeches.
tips: The stream of Vallforners, often known as the stream of Cànoves, is a stream that flows through the municipalities of Tagamanent, Cànoves and Samalús, Cardedeu and Roca del Vallès, where it flows into the Mogent river. In the upper part of Cànoves is the Pantà de Vallforners.

The Vallforners marsh is a reservoir that belongs to the Vallforners creek (Besòs river basin), created by a dam located in the municipality of Cànoves i Samalús, in the region of Vallès Oriental. It was built between 1985 and 1989 and has as a special feature the fact that it was the first swamp in Catalonia whose dam is made of stone and compacted earth.

The Castanyer de Can Cuch (or Castanyer Gros de la Casa del Bosc) is a monumental tree, the largest in Catalonia, with tables and chairs next to it. In 1960, a charcoal burner took advantage of the cavorca of the tree to settle for a year and a half, which gives an idea of the magnitude of the interior of the tree.
Very beautiful hill, with a hermitage that I did not know how to find out. It is in Montseny

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Castanyer Gros de Can Cuch and Vallforners Reservoir Circular trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the trail typically starts from the vicinity of Cànoves i Samalús. Look for designated parking spots in or near the village, which serves as a common access point for routes in this part of Montseny Natural Park.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty, making it suitable for families and children. However, be prepared for some continuous uphill sections, especially alongside the Vallforners stream.

faqs.question: What are the main natural and historical highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse scenery. You'll encounter the serene Vallforners Reservoir, an artificial lake with a unique stone and compacted earth dam. The most distinctive feature is the Castanyer d'en Cuch, a monumental chestnut tree with the largest perimeter in Catalonia, hollow enough to enter. You'll also pass charming architectural points like the Sant Salvador Church and the historic Can Cuch farmhouse.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Castanyer Gros de Can Cuch and Vallforners Reservoir Circular?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 13.2 km long. It typically takes around 4 hours and 14 minutes of moving time to complete, though some sources suggest around 3 hours and 30 minutes without stops. Plan for a bit longer to enjoy the sights.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll walk along the Vallforners Stream, ascend through shady holm oak forests, and traverse some generally sunny slopes. Be prepared for a continuous, humid, shady, and lush uphill climb alongside the river in parts, and a more pronounced ascent when crossing the Torrent de la Baga d'en Cuc. The section bordering the reservoir may lack shade.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. As it's located within Montseny Natural Park, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash to protect wildlife and respect other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal for hiking this trail, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful foliage. Summer can be warm, especially in unshaded sections, while winter might bring colder conditions. The trail's shaded sections along the stream provide some respite during warmer months.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or Montseny Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to enter Montseny Natural Park for day visits. However, always check the official Montseny Natural Park website for any updated regulations or specific restrictions, especially during peak seasons or for certain activities.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the El Meridià Verd, Riera de Vallfornes, Camí de l'Ermita de Sant Salvador, and Camí del Castanyer Gros d'en Cuc, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any places to refresh or get water along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers opportunities to refresh at the Fuentes de las Acàcies and de la Moixera. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water, especially on warmer days, but these natural springs can be a welcome stop.

faqs.question: Can I extend my hike beyond the main circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, hikers have the option to extend their journey. You could venture towards the solitary Suí peak or explore more of the Vallforners Stream from Cànoves, offering additional exploration opportunities within the Montseny Natural Park.
